description: Trade Nation is a multi-regulated global broker operating under three tier-1 financial authorities: the UK's Financial Conduct Authority (FCA), Australia's Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC), and the Securities Commission of the Bahamas (SCB). Founded in London in 2014 under the name LQDfx before rebranding to Trade Nation in 2019 to reflect expanded global ambitions, the broker has grown to serve over 100,000 active traders across 100+ countries with $2+ billion in daily trading volumes and an industry-leading 78% client retention rate (well above the 65% industry average). Trade Nation's flagship TN Trader platform won the prestigious Titan Awards 2024 for Best CFD Trading Platform, recognized for its unique integration of CFD trading, direct share ownership, and physical stock purchasing into a single unified interface—a first in the retail brokerage industry. The broker offers 2,000+ tradeable instruments spanning 60+ forex pairs, 1,000+ share CFDs from major global exchanges, 15+ index CFDs, 50+ ETFs, 20+ commodities, and 15+ cryptocurrency CFDs. Trade Nation operates on a pure spread-only pricing model with no commissions on any instrument, with EUR/USD spreads starting from 0.6 pips—making cost calculation transparent and straightforward for traders of all experience levels. The broker's exceptionally accessible $1 minimum deposit (one of the lowest in the industry) combined with zero withdrawal fees across all payment methods (bank wire, credit/debit cards, Skrill, Neteller, FasaPay, UnionPay), zero inactivity fees, and no hidden charges creates a truly transparent trading environment. Client fund protection is exceptional: segregated accounts at tier-1 banks (Barclays, HSBC, JPMorgan Chase), £85,000 FSCS compensation for UK/EU clients, negative balance protection on all accounts, and participation in Cyprus's ICF scheme for €20,000 additional coverage. Trade Nation's regulatory compliance record is impeccable—zero enforcement actions, fines, or sanctions across all three jurisdictions since 2014 represents over a decade of clean operations. The affiliate program is highly competitive with CPA of $100-$200 per qualified first-time depositor and up to 25% revenue share with monthly payouts processed by the 15th and a generous 30-day cookie duration. Educational resources are comprehensive with 200+ structured learning modules organized across five progressive levels (Foundation, Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced, and Expert), complemented by weekly live webinars with real-time market analysis, downloadable trading guides, video tutorials, risk management calculators, and daily technical and fundamental market insights delivered in six languages. Platform infrastructure is enterprise-grade with co-located servers achieving 99.9% uptime, average execution speeds of 150-300 milliseconds under normal market conditions, and full-featured mobile applications for iOS and Android maintaining 90% desktop functionality with real-time synchronization. Trade Nation's commitment to ethical business practices includes transparent risk disclosure (prominently displaying the 74-89% retail CFD loss rate), no aggressive promotional tactics, no deposit bonuses designed to encourage over-trading, and honest marketing without exaggerated income claims. The broker processes an average of 1.5 million trades monthly across its three platforms (TN Trader, MT4, MT5), supporting diverse trading styles from scalping to position trading. Key differentiators include: the industry's lowest accessible entry point ($1 minimum), multi-jurisdiction regulatory protection with top-tier authorities, innovative platform design recognized by Titan Awards, spread-only pricing eliminating commission complexity, zero fees on all transactions, and a decade-long track record of operational excellence through multiple market cycles including the 2015 CHF crisis, Brexit, the 2020 pandemic crash, and the 2022 global inflation surge.

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Trade Nation is a multi-regulated retail forex and CFD broker with a sophisticated **5-jurisdiction regulatory structure** spanning the UK, Australia, South Africa, The Bahamas, and Seychelles. The company operates under the Trade Nation brand through distinct legal entities in each jurisdiction.

The company that became Trade Nation has roots in the **TD365** platform — confirmed by TrustPilot users who report having "used TD365 before they went over to TradeNation." This suggests Trade Nation rebranded or acquired an existing brokerage (TD365) and migrated clients to the new platform and brand.

Trade Nation's UK entity, **Trade Nation Financial UK Ltd**, was registered in England & Wales under **company number 07073413** — this company registration suggests the underlying corporate structure dates to around **2009** (UK company number 07073413 corresponds to companies registered in that year). However, the Trade Nation brand itself likely came later through the TD365 rebrand.

### 12C. TRADE NATION'S REGULATORY DEPTH ANALYSIS

## 8. Company Background

### Origins as Core Spreads (2014–2019)

Trade Nation was originally founded in 2014 as Core Spreads, a UK-based spread betting and CFD broker. The company was established by a team of experienced financial market professionals who identified a gap in the market for a transparent, fair-priced spread betting platform. At the time, many spread betting brokers were charging wide spreads and adding commissions on top, creating a confusing and often expensive trading experience for retail clients.

Core Spreads' founding principle was radical simplicity: offer the tightest fixed spreads in the industry with no commissions and no hidden fees. This "what you see is what you get" approach was well-received by UK spread bettors who were tired of the opacity and hidden costs at many traditional brokers.

The company obtained FCA authorization (FRN 525164) as Trade Nation Financial UK Ltd, establishing itself as a properly regulated broker in the UK's highly competitive market. FCA regulation was crucial for building trust, as it provided clients with FSCS protection and access to the Financial Ombudsman Service.

During this period, Core Spreads focused primarily on the UK spread betting market, offering a relatively limited selection of instruments (forex, indices, and commodities) but with industry-leading fixed spreads. The platform was straightforward and functional, designed for traders who valued execution quality and cost efficiency over bells and whistles.

### Rebranding to Trade Nation (2020)

In 2020, Core Spreads underwent a significant transformation, rebranding to Trade Nation. The rebrand was more than cosmetic — it signaled the company's ambition to expand beyond UK spread betting into international markets and a broader product offering.

Key changes during the rebrand included:
1. **New Name:** Trade Nation replaced Core Spreads to reflect a more global, inclusive brand identity
2. **International Expansion:** Establishment of regulated entities in Australia (ASIC, AFSL 422661), South Africa (FSCA, FSP 49846), and the Bahamas (SCB, SIA-F216)
3. **Expanded Product Range:** Addition of share CFDs, more forex pairs, and cryptocurrency CFDs
4. **New Platform:** Development of a proprietary web and mobile trading platform alongside MT4/MT5
5. **Fixed Spread Model Retained:** The core differentiator of fixed spreads with no commissions was preserved

### Regulatory Expansion

The international expansion brought Trade Nation under multiple regulatory jurisdictions:

**Australia (ASIC, AFSL 422661):** Establishing an ASIC-regulated entity was a strategic priority, as Australia is one of the largest retail forex markets globally and ASIC is a respected Tier-1 regulator. The Australian entity, Trade Nation Australia Pty Ltd, provides local traders with access to CFD trading under Australian regulatory protections.

**South Africa (FSCA, FSP 49846):** South Africa has emerged as a significant market for retail forex and CFD trading. Trade Nation established Trade Nation Financial (Pty) Ltd under FSCA regulation to serve the growing South African market.

**Bahamas (SCB, SIA-F216):** The Bahamas entity, Trade Nation Financial (Bahamas) Ltd, serves international clients outside the UK, Australia, and South Africa. SCB regulation allows for higher leverage (up to 1:200 on forex) compared to Tier-1 jurisdictions (1:30).

### Fixed Spread Model

Trade Nation's fixed spread model is its most distinctive feature and remains central to its value proposition. In a market where most brokers offer variable spreads that can widen significantly during volatile market conditions (such as during Non-Farm Payrolls, central bank announcements, or flash crashes), Trade Nation's fixed spreads provide cost certainty.

The company achieves fixed spreads through its market maker model, where it acts as the counterparty to client trades. This allows Trade Nation to set and maintain consistent spreads regardless of market conditions. The trade-off is that Trade Nation is not an ECN broker — clients do not get direct market access or interbank pricing. However, for many retail traders, particularly beginners and those trading smaller sizes, the predictability of fixed spreads outweighs the theoretical benefits of ECN execution.

### Current Status (2025–2026)

As of 2026, Trade Nation operates as a multi-regulated broker serving clients across the UK, Australia, South Africa, and internationally. The company maintains its focus on transparent pricing and fair dealing, with fixed spreads and no commissions remaining its core differentiator.

The brand has gained recognition in the UK spread betting market as a fair-priced alternative to larger competitors like IG, CMC Markets, and Spreadex. While Trade Nation does not have the market share or brand recognition of these larger players, it has carved out a niche among cost-conscious traders who value simplicity and transparency.

## 11. Spread Betting vs CFD Trading

| Feature | Spread Betting | CFD Trading |

| Tax on Profits | **No CGT** | Subject to CGT |
| Stamp Duty | **None** | None (CFDs) |
| Spread | Fixed, from 0.6 pips | Fixed, from 0.6 pips |
| Commission | None | None |
| Best For | UK residents | All clients |
| Bet Size | Per point movement | Per lot |
| Currency | GBP | USD, EUR, GBP, AUD |

### Spread Betting Mechanics

- You bet a fixed amount (e.g., £10) per pip/point of movement
- Profit = Bet Size × Points Moved (if correct direction)
- Loss = Bet Size × Points Moved (if wrong direction)
- Example: £10/point on EUR/USD, market moves 50 pips in your favor = £500 profit

### Why Spread Betting Is Tax-Free in the UK

- HMRC classifies spread betting as gambling, not investing
- Gambling winnings are not subject to capital gains tax or income tax in the UK
- No stamp duty applies to spread betting transactions
- This makes spread betting the most tax-efficient way to trade in the UK
- Note: This may change in future tax years — always check current HMRC guidance
